title: dianthus fruticosus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Dianthus fruticosus subsp. occidentalis, also known as fragrant pink rockrose, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family, native to the western Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including its ability to reduce stress, improve sleep, and protect against oxidative stress, due to its antioxidant properties. Dianthus fruticosus subsp. occidentalis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
